item.title: School Swimming Championships: SVC and Drasa Avenue win boys' and girls' divisions
Valladolid 7s: Fijiana 7s beat South Africa 17-12
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 30/05/2026 The Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s side defeated South Africa 17-12 in their last match of the World Championship Valladolid 7s tournament tonight.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 30/05/2026

The Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s side defeated South Africa 17-12 in their last match of the World Championship Valladolid 7s tournament tonight.

Atelaite Buna Ralivanawa, Rogosau Adimereani and Reapi Ulunisau scored a try each to help the Fijiana 7s side to book a place in the playoffs.

South Africa try came from Byrhandre Dolf and Maria Tshiremba.

item.title: Taylor powers through injury to claim multiple gold medals at Swimming Championships
More elderly Fijians being abandoned and neglected - HART Home
item.description: Nursing homes are running out of space as more elderly Fijians are being neglected by their families and abandoned at hospitals, and calls are being made for the Government to build more aged-care facilities. While speaking to fijivillage News, Valelevu HART Home staff matron Sereima Rabi says the home currently has 26 residents and is running out of space, while continuing to receive requests fro
item.content:encoded:
Nursing homes are running out of space as more elderly Fijians are being neglected by their families and abandoned at hospitals, and calls are being made for the Government to build more aged-care facilities.

While speaking to fijivillage News, Valelevu HART Home staff matron Sereima Rabi says the home currently has 26 residents and is running out of space, while continuing to receive requests from families seeking care for their elderly parents.

She says they have been receiving calls for weeks and months about elderly patients who are ready to be discharged from hospital, but whose families cannot be reached or fail to come and pick them up.

The staff matron says it is heartbreaking to see what is happening to senior citizens, as many residents in the home miss their families, feel lonely and receive little or no contact from their loved ones.

Rabi adds that some residents have been living at the home for up to 10 years, while others have only recently arrived, and the facility also cares for people with disabilities, dementia, stroke-related conditions and those who are bedridden.

The matron says there are many elderly people living on the streets as well.

She says they have seen an increase in the number of elderly people being placed in nursing homes, which she attributes largely to family neglect, adding that some elderly parents are being mistreated or abandoned by their own children and relatives.

The matron says they are expanding the HART Nursing Home to accommodate more people but that is going to take about three months so they have been referring them to other homes but they are also running out of space.

Rabi is pleading with families to look after their elderly as most of the nursing homes are completely occupied.

item.title: Central College student crowned Student Designer of the Year at Fiji Water Fashion Show
item.description: Central College student Titilia Grace has been crowned Student Designer of the Year at the Fiji Fashion Week Student Designer Show Presented by Fiji Water, impressing judges with a collection inspired by nature and Fiji Water. Grace, who hails from Tailevu, says she never expected to win the top award and was shocked when her name was announced.
item.content:encoded:
Central College student Titilia Grace has been crowned Student Designer of the Year at the Fiji Fashion Week Student Designer Show Presented by Fiji Water, impressing judges with a collection inspired by nature and Fiji Water.

Grace, who hails from Tailevu, says she never expected to win the top award and was shocked when her name was announced.

Speaking to fijivillage News, she says one of her biggest challenges was finding the money to buy materials for her designs, and she is grateful to those who supported and sponsored her journey.

Grace says her collection, Floral Hazel, carried the theme Blooming Serenity and was inspired by nature and the colour green, while one design was inspired by Fiji Water.

She is encouraging young Fijians interested in fashion design to never give up and to follow their passion.

The Fiji Water Student Fashion Designer Show was held at the Vodafone Arena, where 15 student designers showcased their creations on the runway.

Dudley High School finished as first runner-up, while Natabua High School was named second runner-up.

Managing Director of Fiji Fashion Week Ellen Whippy-Knight thanked Fiji Water for sponsoring the event and for committing its support to the student designer programme for the next three years.

The show also recognised outstanding models, with Samuel Waqaniburotukula of Marist Brothers High School winning Best Male Model, while Adi Luse Naivalu of Natabua High School took out the Best Female Model award.

Students showcased their creative designs on the runway, highlighting their talent, creativity and confidence.

The Vodafone Fiji Fashion Week Resort Luxe Show will begin at 6pm at the Vodafone Arena.

item.title: JP calls for vigilance after falling victim to viber scam
item.description: Justice of the Peace and Nasinu Nari Sabha President Lalita Kumari was devastated to discover that her Viber account had been hacked by overseas scammers, resulting in more than $1,000 being fraudulently obtained using her name. Speaking to fijivillage News following the Public Consultation on Online Safety in Samabula today, Kumari says she w as shocked to become a victim of such a scam.
item.content:encoded:
Justice of the Peace and Nasinu Nari Sabha President Lalita Kumari was devastated to discover that her Viber account had been hacked by overseas scammers, resulting in more than $1,000 being fraudulently obtained using her name.

Speaking to fijivillage News following the Public Consultation on Online Safety in Samabula today, Kumari says she w as shocked to become a victim of such a scam.

She revealed that the incident occurred while she was in New Zealand and described the experience as both surprising and distressing.

Kumari says her online accounts have been targeted on multiple occasions and that she has raised her concerns with the Online Safety Commission.

She is now urging the public to remain vigilant and take precautions to protect themselves from online scams and cybercrime.

item.title: Dog shoots woman with shotgun at Nebraska convenience store
item.description: Police responding to reports of a shotgun blast at a convenience store sounds like the opening scene of many American crime films, but when officers in Nebraska attended a recent incident, they discovered an unusual culprit: a dog. Local television station KNOP News 2 reported that police in the town of Scottsbluff were called to a convenience store following reports of a shotgun being discharged.
item.content:encoded:
Police responding to reports of a shotgun blast at a convenience store sounds like the opening scene of many American crime films, but when officers in Nebraska attended a recent incident, they discovered an unusual culprit: a dog.

Local television station KNOP News 2 reported that police in the town of Scottsbluff were called to a convenience store following reports of a shotgun being discharged.

When officers arrived, they found a truck with damage to one of its doors and a woman who had been struck in the arm by a shotgun pellet.

However, investigations revealed the shooting was caused by a dog. The vehicle had pulled up outside the store when the dog was moving across the back seat.

The dog somehow triggered the shotgun, which had a live round chambered, causing it to discharge. The blast damaged the vehicle and struck a woman nearby.

The victim was taken to hospital but did not suffer serious injuries.

It is illegal in Nebraska to drive with a loaded shotgun in a vehicle. Source: The Guardian

item.title: Lawyer admits misconduct after failing to appear for client
Fiji Fashion Week sold out ahead of tonight's showcase
item.description: What was shaping up to be one of the toughest years for Vodafone Fiji Fashion Week has turned into a success story, with tickets now completely sold out ahead of tonight's event. Managing Director Ellen Whippy-Knight says ticket sales this year were among the slowest the event has experienced in its 19-year history.
item.content:encoded:
What was shaping up to be one of the toughest years for Vodafone Fiji Fashion Week has turned into a success story, with tickets now completely sold out ahead of tonight's event.

Managing Director Ellen Whippy-Knight says ticket sales this year were among the slowest the event has experienced in its 19-year history.

She attributed the slow uptake partly to global economic pressures and the timing of the Ratu Sukuna long weekend, which she believes has drawn many expatriates away during the Fiji Fashion Week period over the past three years.

However, a last-minute marketing push supported by sponsors and media partners including Vodafone Fiji, and Communications Fiji Limited helped revive ticket sales.

Whippy-Knight says organisers were so overwhelmed by demand that they gathered to rearrange seating and create additional space for guests.

She describes the sell-out as a major boost for designers and audiences alike, saying Fiji Fashion Week continues to provide an important platform for local and international fashion talent while connecting designers directly with potential buyers.

This year’s event will feature 15 designers, including six international designers and nine local creatives.

Whippy-Knight says some designers had to be removed from the programme due to scheduling changes, but stressed the decisions were not based on talent or skill .

She says priority was given to designers who had already purchased tickets and accommodation for the event.

The fashion awards will once again be incorporated into the main show programme after returning to the runway format last year instead of being held as a separate event.

Whippy-Knight also announced the arrival of international models Madeline, Anya, Eve and Ashley, along with model coordinator Gary Manuel, saying overseas talent helps elevate the standard of the show while offering local models valuable exposure and learning opportunities.

The Vodafone Fiji Fashion Week show will start at 6 this evening at the Vodafone Arena.

item.title: Kawakawa, Donu ban starts from Monday, Beche-de-mer harvest period extended
Fiji eyes unbeaten pool run against hosts Spain
item.description: Following a narrow defeat to France and a win against the USA, Spain will be eager to make a statement on home soil when they take on the Fiji Airways Men's National 7s side in their final pool match of the World Championship in Valladolid, however, head coach Osea Kolinisau says Fiji is not underestimating any team. Kolinisau had stressed before the tournament that his side would take one game at
item.content:encoded:
Following a narrow defeat to France and a win against the USA, Spain will be eager to make a statement on home soil when they take on the Fiji Airways Men's National 7s side in their final pool match of the World Championship in Valladolid, however, head coach Osea Kolinisau says Fiji is not underestimating any team.

Kolinisau had stressed before the tournament that his side would take one game at a time and focus on the task at hand as the competition progresses.

After beating France 26-12 and the USA 19-7, Fiji has already secured a place in the Cup quarter-finals.

The team will be looking to maintain its unbeaten record in the tournament while keeping an eye on the bigger challenges that lie ahead in the knockout stages.

Fiji will next play hosts Spain at 12.12am tomorrow.

The Cup quarterfinals are at 2.27am tomorrow.

The Cup semi finals are at 8.44pm tomorrow before the Cup final kicks off at 2.26am Monday.

Meanwhile, the Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s side suffered a 26-19 defeat to the USA before being thrashed 45-5 by Australia in their opening two pool matches. They take on South Africa at 8 tonight.

The Women's cup quarter finals are at 12.49am tomorrow.

The cup semifinals are at 8pm tomorrow, while the Women's cup final kicks off at 1.46am Monday.

item.title: Valladolid 7s: Fiji through to Cup quarters after beating USA 19-7
item.description: The Fiji Airways Men's National 7s side are through to the Cup quarterfinals after beating the USA 19-7 in their second pool game at the World Championship in Valladolid, Spain. Fiji had thumped France 26-12 in their first game.
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji Airways Men's National 7s side are through to the Cup quarterfinals after beating the USA 19-7 in their second pool game at the World Championship in Valladolid, Spain.

Fiji had thumped France 26-12 in their first game.

George Bose scored first for Fiji after they overpowered the USA scrum to lead 7-0.

The USA pulled one back through Orrin Bizer to level things 7-7 at half time.

Filipe Sauturaga then sprinted coast to coast untouched after the USA lost the ball inside Fiji' 10 metre line to regain the lead 14-7.

After that the Fiji team went to work tearing apart the USA defence before skipper Terio Veilawa side stepped two players before unselfishly passing to Tanivanuakula to score and make it 19-7.

Fiji will next play hosts Spain at 12.12am tomorrow.

The Cup quarterfinals are at 2.27am tomorrow.

The Cup semi finals are at 8.44pm tomorrow before the Cup final kicks off at 2.26am Monday.

In other games, Spain beat the USA 26-21, New Zealand thumped Germany 33-12, Argentina thrashed Uruguay 40-14, Australia defeated Great Britain 26-12, while South Africa beat Kenya 14-0.

item.title: Lomaiviti sounds alarm on drugs as leaders push to bring back village levies
Leadership Fiji and Eco Clean renew partnership for another three years
